element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Purchasing weapon upgrades (boomerang gun) and items
Player must choose direction; 40+ possible routes available

element_introduction_order.element: Force powers (shrinking, teleportation, flight, healing, invincibility)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Acquired through exploration and progression; unlock different areas
Primary objective; required to control Doomsday Bomb; distributed across world
Teaches Bang various Force powers upon rescue
Initially trusted ally; later revealed as demon-controlled antagonist

element_introduction_order.element: Mental discomfort / Demon mind control attempt
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Failed mind control by demon beneath mountain; foreshadows final conflict
Treasure trap that would free imprisoned demon; must avoid or reverse
Only weapon capable of destroying the demon; required for true ending
Time-pressure mechanic; must place 6 medallions before countdown expires
Aliens created humanity 1000 years ago; intend destruction due to human violence

typical_relief_triggers: Successful shop visit and equipment upgrade
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering small enemy groups requiring handgun combat
Exploration of new routes with unknown dangers
Facing the demon (MadGazer) with inadequate preparation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Open-ended exploration with escalating stakes; non-linear pacing allows player to build tension gradually through self-directed questing; final stage imposes strict time pressure through countdown mechanic

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Backtracking to previously-visited routes; off-path exploration leads to hidden medallions and power-ups
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Magic Stone trap hidden as treasure reward; demon imprisonment location at northern end of mountain requires deliberate exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty; basic handgun combat against small enemies; player learns shop system and overworld navigation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty; encounters with governors increase enemy complexity; Force power acquisition opens new areas; mental discomfort theme introduces puzzle elements
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; demon boss fight requires Sword of Apollo (hidden item); bomb defusal countdown creates time pressure; incorrect medallion placement triggers bad ending
notable_spikes: First encounter with Tom Guycot (introduces medallion objective complexity)
Discovery that Michael is mind-controlled (narrative twist requires strategy recalibration)
Doomsday Bomb countdown sequence (time pressure spike; guessing game with world-ending stakes)
Demon battle with inadequate Sword of Apollo access (punishes incomplete exploration)

notable_level_patterns.description: Routes containing governor enemies protecting medallions; each victory removes one control source for the Doomsday Bomb; distributed across overworld to encourage non-linear exploration
Hidden routes containing Hermit and other key NPCs requiring rescue; rescues unlock Force powers that gate access to further areas; creates progression locks disguised as optional content
Northern end of Demonhead mountain contains demon imprisonment; exploration here triggers mental discomfort and eventual confrontation with possessed Michael; represents culmination of foreshadowing

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Bomb Defusal Puzzle with Time Pressure
notable_level_patterns.description: Final stage forces placement of 6 medallions into bomb control system with active countdown; player must guess correct placement with no instructions; failure triggers bad ending (world destruction); success triggers good ending with alien departure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Final bomb chamber / Route position unknown
